titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Edward Taylor collection
titlestmt.author: Karen Spicher

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday July 20, 2019 at 03:02

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

langmaterial.language: In English and Latin. Some materials are in shorthand.

did.abstract: The Edward Taylor Collection contains nine volumes of autograph manuscript writings, including poems, sermons, notes on divinity, and commonplace books; and fragments of writings, correspondence, and other materials removed from bindings of these volumes. Some volumes include autograph manuscript ownership inscriptions and other annotations by Taylor's son-in-law Isaac Stiles and grandson Ezra Stiles.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Writings, 1670s-1729. II. Materials Removed from Bindings, 1670s-1767.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material is open for research.

Box 3 ("China's description" and commonplace book): Restricted fragile material. A reference surrogate is available in Beinecke Library's Digital images Online database. For further information consult he appropriate curator.

Items requiring conservation treatment: Restricted fragile material. These items are identified by temporary removal forms filed in the collection. Microfilm reference surrogates may be available. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

otherfindaid.head: Other Finding Aids
otherfindaid.p: For page-level subject indexing of materials formerly part of the Ezra Stiles Papers, see A Guide to the Microfilm Edition of the Ezra Stiles Papers at Yale University

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: Materials formerly part of the Ezra Stiles Papers: available on microfilm from Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Box 5 (Commonplace book): Gift of Lewis Stiles Gannett, 1961.
Box 10 (Poems): Gift of Henry Taylor. Terry, 1921.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Edward Taylor Collection. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

bioghist.head: Edward Taylor (1642-1729) (Harvard 1671)
bioghist.p: Edward Taylor (1642-1729) (Harvard 1671), Congregational clergyman and poet. Taylor was minister of the First Congregational Church (Westfield, Mass.), 1671-1729. He married Ruth Wyllys (1670-1730) in 1692; their daughter Kezia Taylor Stiles (1702-1727) married Isaac Stiles (1697-1760) (Yale 1722), minister in North Haven, Connecticut, and their son Ezra Stiles (1727-1795) (Yale 1746), was minister of the Second Congregational Church, Newport, Rhode Island, 1755-1776, and president of Yale College, 1777-1795.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Edward Taylor Collection contains nine volumes of autograph manuscript writings, including poems, sermons, notes on divinity, and commonplace books; and fragments of writings, correspondence, and other materials removed from bindings of these volumes. Some volumes include autograph manuscript ownership inscriptions and other annotations by Taylor's son-in-law Isaac Stiles and grandson Ezra Stiles.

Detailed descriptions of the following volumes are in The Poems of Edward Taylor , edited by Donald E. Stanford (New Haven: Yale University Press, 1960), in Appendix 2. Manuscripts:
"China's description" and commonplace book
"Christographia"
Dispensatory
Poems
Poetical works, and materials removed from binding.

bioghist.head: The Taylor family
bioghist.p: The following is a partial genealogy, showing descendants of Edward Taylor. Donors of contents of the Taylor Collection are indicated by italics.
---------------(4)Henry Wyllys Taylor (1796-1888) (Yale 1816) m. (1832) Martha Caldwell Masters (1808-1884)

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection consists of materials formerly classed as Ms Vault Taylor, Ms Vault 9:1, Z117 0201, Z117 201, Z117 202 1, Z117 202 2, Z117 202 3, Z117 203, Z117 204, Z117 205, Z117 206, Z117 207, and Z117 208. Some of these materials were included in the microfilm edition of the Ezra Stiles Papers and are indexed in A Guide to the Microfilm Edition of the Ezra Stiles Papers at Yale University , edited by Harold E. Selesky (New Haven: Yale University Library, 1978). Before processing in 2018, readers were generally required to access these materials via the microfilm, and each microfilm frame included a notice requiring permission of the Yale University Library for reproduction or publication. Prior to microfilming, some volumes were disbound, and manuscript fragments removed from bindings were housed separately. Some contents of the collection were treated by conservators during custody by Yale or possibly by former owners, including silking of fragile paper.

In 2018, the collection was reclassed under one call number and organized in two series. Index cards used as microfilm targets were discarded. A fragile volume titled "China's description and commonplace book" was disbound and treated by conservators, and its binding was rehoused separately. Other fragile and silked items were referred to conservators for evaluation and treatment. Other than restricted access to fragile materials, all access, reproduction, and publication restrictions were lifted.

odd.head: Publications
odd.p: The Poems of Edward Taylor , edited by Donald E. Stanford (New Haven: Yale University Press, 1960), Appendix 2. Manuscripts.

subject.part: Congregational churches -- Clergy -- Early works to 1800
Congregational churches -- Doctrines -- Early works to 1800

scopecontent.p: Includes annotations by Ezra Stiles (1727-1795) (Yale 1746)

accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. A reference surrogate is available in Beinecke Library's Digital images Online database. For further information consult he appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes autograph manuscript notes by Isaac Stiles (1697-1760) (Yale 1722); and autograph manuscript notes by Ezra Stiles (1727-1795) (Yale 1746) on his degree from Edinburgh University, Benjamin Franklin, and Stiles family genealogy

scopecontent.p: Includes "Memoir of Revd. Mr. Taylor, " and other notes, autograph manuscript, by Ezra Stiles (1727-1795) (Yale 1746)

scopecontent.p: Includes brief notes on writings on medicine by Lazare Rivière

relatedmaterial.p: Other versions of some poems are in Poetical works

did.unittitle: Printed fragments from Luke 23 and prologue to John , from an unidentified Bible
Accounts; list of church members, Westfield, Massachusetts; and unidentified notes
List of clothes; and fragments of herbals
Manuscript letter fragment, from an unidentified writer, Boston
Penmanship exercises and notes in unidentified hands
Autograph letter, signed, from John Pynchon, Jr., to Edward Taylor
Autograph letter, draft, from Edward Taylor to Solomon Stoddard
Notes on ages of people in the Bible; and catechism fragments in an unidentfied hand

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Catechism fragments: possibly a revision of The First Principles of the Doctrine of Christ , by John Fitch (Boston: printed by John Foster, 1679)

did.unittitle: Autograph letter, signed, from Daniel Hooker to Edward Taylor
Metrical paraphrases of Psalms I-V and Psalms VII-IX

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pages [31]-[46]: removed from Dispensatory

did.unittitle: Metrical paraphrases of Psalms XIII-XXXVIII , three verses of Psalm XXXIX , Psalm XLIX , and heading of Psalm L
Lock of "Isaac Stiles's hair," 1767 February 28; and two unidentified locks of hair
Two fragments from unidentified metrical paraphrases; Two fragments in an unidentifed hand; and two spine fragments from binding
Unidentified printed fragments on Latin rhetoric
Unidentified printed fragment on English grammar
Unidentified printed fragment on English grammar
Fragment of an autograph letter, signed, from John Pynchon, Jr., to Edward Taylor; with notes in shorthand by Taylor

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Former call number: Z117 208
